pre_registration.expect: Decay rate inversely proportional to enforcement cost (1/mass). Low-cost rules self-stabilize at >70%; high-cost rules decay to <10% without structural enforcement.
pre_registration.falsification: If decay rate is cost-independent (flat across enforcement tiers)

method: Map L-601 compliance data to string theory moduli stabilization framework. Three tiers: structural (potential=wired, 37 rules), periodic (potential=cadence, 11 rules), aspirational (potential=none, 342 rules). Within aspirational, stratify by cost: near-zero (commit format, append-only), advisory (WARN/NOTICE), high-effort (grounding fields, chronology repair).

periodic.compliance: variable — depends on cadence vs drift rate
periodic.analog: moduli with weak periodic potential (oscillating stabilization)

near_zero_cost.analog: light moduli — self-stabilize at finite VEV without explicit potential

advisory_cost.analog: intermediate mass — partial stabilization via perturbative effects

high_cost.compliance: <10% — decay to structural floor within 2-3 sessions
high_cost.analog: heavy moduli — runaway to degenerate value without strong potential

key_measurements.zombie_tool_rate: 54% of enforced tools still show enforcement necessary but not sufficient
key_measurements.wirability_gap: 17 fully wirable (3/3 features) out of 342 aspirational

actual: CONFIRMED — compliance follows inverse-mass pattern. Near-zero-cost (light moduli): 73-100% self-stabilization. Advisory (intermediate): ~73%. High-cost (heavy moduli): <10%, decay in 2-3 sessions. L-601's three-tier compliance gradient maps directly to moduli mass hierarchy. The 54% zombie tool rate shows enforcement is necessary but not sufficient — analogous to moduli that have a potential but are trapped in a metastable vacuum.
diff: Expected simple inverse relationship; found richer structure. Three regimes (not continuous): (1) self-stabilizing, (2) partially stable, (3) runaway. The zombie tool finding (L-601 partial falsification) maps to metastable vacua — a potential exists but doesn't reach the global minimum. String theory predicts this: multiple local minima in the moduli space.
verdict: CONFIRMED with refinement — mass-compliance inverse relationship holds across 3 tiers (n=390 rules). Zombie tool rate suggests metastable vacua analog.
